title: Missouri Abolition Bill Author Mike Moon Elected to State Senate
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States reports that term-limited Missouri State Rep. Mike Moon, author of the total-abolition bill MO HB2285, won the Republican primary for Senate District 29 and intends to refile his bill in the Senate in 2021. The piece frames Moon's victory as both a political win and an indictment of pro-life incrementalism that has kept abortion legal in Missouri.

questions.question: What political race did Mike Moon win, and what does the result mean for his legislative future?
questions.answer: Moon, a four-term state representative who hit his term limit in the House, won the Republican primary for Senate District 29 by defeating former Missouri GOP chairman David Cole. With no Democrat on the ballot, the primary win effectively secured him a seat in the Missouri State Senate beginning in 2021.
questions.quote: Moon defeated former Missouri Republican Party Chairman David Cole 52.5% to 47.5% to become the Senate District 29 Republican nominee. There is no Democrat running for the seat so Moon will join the State Senate in 2021.

questions.question: What was MO HB2285 and who joined Moon in authoring it?
questions.answer: HB2285 was Moon's bill of total and immediate abolition introduced in January 2020. Five other Republican representatives co-authored it: Dirk Deaton, Ben Baker, Jeff Pogue, Tom Hurst, and Brad Hudson.
questions.quote: In January, Moon introduced MO HB2285, a bill of total and immediate abolition. It was co-authored by Reps. Dirk Deaton, Ben Baker, Jeff Pogue, Tom Hurst, and Brad Hudson.

questions.question: Why did the bill fail to advance in the 2020 session, and who is responsible?
questions.answer: Republican Rep. Sheila Solon, the chair of the House Children and Families committee and an endorsee of Missouri Right to Life, blocked the bill from receiving a hearing. Free the States holds her personally responsible for keeping abortion legal in Missouri for another year.
questions.quote: Missouri House Children and Families committee chair Rep. Sheila Solon, a pro-life Republican endorsed by Missouri Right to Life in 2018, denied the bill a committee hearing, keeping abortion legal in the state for another year.

questions.question: What hypocrisy in current Missouri statute does Moon's bill target?
questions.answer: Existing Missouri law begins by declaring the state a 'sanctuary of life' that defends the right to life of the unborn, then immediately undercuts that declaration by saying the state will merely regulate abortion to whatever extent the U.S. Supreme Court allows. Moon's bill strips out that surrender clause.
questions.quote: It clarifies that Missouri is actually the opposite of a sanctuary for unborn children; that the state will not defend the right to life but will “regulate” the killing of preborn children if given permission to do so by “decisions of the United States Supreme Court.”

questions.question: How does Moon's bill rewrite the relevant statute?
questions.answer: It replaces 'Regulate abortion to the full extent permitted by the Constitution of the United States, decisions of the United States Supreme Court, and federal statutes' with a simple directive to 'Abolish abortion in this state.'
questions.quote: (3) ~~Regulate~~ **Abolish** abortion ~~to the full extent permitted by the Constitution of the United States, decisions of the United States Supreme Court, and federal statutes~~ in this state.

questions.question: How does the bill address the authority of federal court rulings like Roe?
questions.answer: The bill explicitly nullifies any federal act, ruling, or regulation that would override Missouri's abolition of abortion, treating Roe-style precedent as void under both the Missouri and U.S. Constitutions.
questions.quote: Any federal act, law, treaty, decision, order, rule, or regulation that purports to supersede, stay, or overrule section A of this act is in violation of the Constitution of the state of Missouri and the Constitution of the United States and is therefore void.

questions.question: How does the article respond to the claim that abolition bills mandate prosecuting all women who have abortions for murder?
questions.answer: Silberman calls Cole's claim false. He argues that, just as with crimes against born humans, circumstances determine whether and how a mother is prosecuted. A woman coerced into an abortion under threat would not be prosecuted; abolition simply equalizes legal protection for preborn and born children.
questions.quote: If someone is coerced into having an abortion under threat of bodily harm, they would not be prosecuted, just like any other crime committed against one’s will. There is nothing in Moon’s or any other abolition bill that specifies that a mother is to be prosecuted for murder no matter the circumstances.

questions.question: What did Dr. Wes Scroggins argue against pro-life incrementalism in his letter to the editor?
questions.answer: Scroggins, a Missouri State University professor, argued that 'pro-life' regulatory legislation has cost more lives than it has saved by tolerating abortion under government regulation, and that the only Biblical, Constitutional, and moral position is total abolition.
questions.quote: If preborn babies are innocent persons created in the image of God, the only Biblical, Constitutional, and moral position that can be taken is the total abolition of abortion. Anything else is an uncompassionate injustice called murder…

questions.question: How did Scroggins frame Moon's bill in terms of an elected official's duty?
questions.answer: Scroggins framed HB2285 as nothing more than Moon honoring his oath of office to uphold the Missouri Constitution, which by the will of the people grants personhood at conception. He challenged readers to ask whether elected officials should not be expected to do exactly what they swore to do.
questions.quote: State Rep. Moon has done nothing more with the filing of HB 2285 than uphold his oath of office to uphold the Missouri Constitution. Again, the Missouri Constitution, by the will of the people of Missouri, grants personhood at conception.

questions.question: How did Moon characterize Cole's strategy of running to Moon's right on abortion?
questions.answer: Moon mocked Cole, a former leader in John Kasich's presidential campaign, for trying to pose as the genuine anti-abortion candidate, calling Cole's posture an unconvincing imitation of Moon himself.
questions.quote: My opponent attempted to purport himself as me during the campaign. I guess the old saying, ‘Imitation is the sincerest form of flattery,’ is true in the case of this campaign.

questions.question: What political conditions does the article say will exist in Missouri in 2021, and what conclusion does it draw?
questions.answer: Missouri is expected to have a Republican governor, attorney general, lieutenant governor, and supermajorities in both chambers. With Moon in the Senate carrying an abolition bill, the article argues the only thing that could keep abortion legal is pro-life officials again refusing to legislate what they claim to believe.
questions.quote: There’s no reason that murdering preborn children would still be legal in Missouri this time next year unless, as Dr. Scroggins put it, Missouri’s pro-life elected official continue refusing to legislate according to what they say they believe and what they swear to uphold.

related.title: Missouri is not Abortion-Free. But Thanks to Sen. Mike Moon, it Can Be.
related.reason: Direct follow-up announcing Moon's 2021 Senate version of the abolition bill referenced at the end of this article.

related.title: Rally to SHOW ME Abolition in Missouri on March 10
related.reason: Mobilization event tied to Moon's 2021 'Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act,' the Senate sequel to HB2285.

related.title: Hearing for Abolition of Abortion in Missouri Act Scheduled For Wednesday 3/10
related.reason: Reports the committee hearing Moon's reintroduced bill finally received in the Senate, contrasting with Solon's 2020 blockade described here.

related.title: Point of Order! How Missouri Baptists Killed the Resolution on Abolishing Abortion.
related.reason: Continues the Missouri abolition narrative, examining how institutional pro-life forces have undercut total-abolition efforts in the same state.

related.title: Incrementalism Versus Immediatism in a Nutshell
related.reason: Provides the theoretical framework behind Scroggins' and Silberman's critique of 'pro-life' regulation versus total abolition.

related.title: June V. Russo Must Be the Death Knell of Pro-Life Judicial Supremacy and Incrementalism
related.reason: Expands on the article's argument that deferring to Supreme Court precedent (the very clause Moon's bill removes) is a failed strategy.

related.title: Alaska Abolition Bill Author David Eastman Wins Primary
related.reason: Parallel case of an abolition bill author winning a primary, useful context for the broader 2020 wave of abolitionist legislators.
